Class Dragon

java.lang.Object
core.Piece
pieces.Dragon
All Implemented Interfaces:
Moveable, Stabbable
public class Dragon extends Piece implements Stabbable
Represents a highly powerful Dragon piece on the game board. The Dragon behaves similarly to a Queen in standard chess, capable of sliding any number of unoccupied squares in any of the 8 orthogonal or diagonal directions. It is a formidable melee unit that captures enemies by landing on them.

  • Field Summary

  • Constructor Details

    • Dragon

      public Dragon(Color color, Position position)
      Initializes a new Dragon piece.
      Parameters:
      color - the color of the Dragon
      position - the initial starting position

  • Method Details

    • getLegalMoves

      public List<Position> getLegalMoves(Board board)
      Calculates all legal movement destinations for the Dragon. The Dragon slides continuously in all 8 directions until it reaches the edge of the board, an allied piece, or an enemy piece (which it can capture).
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in interface Moveable
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in class Piece
      Parameters:
      board - the current game board
      Returns:
      a list of valid positions the Dragon can move to or capture on

    • stab

      public Piece stab(Position target, Board board)
      Executes a stabbing melee attack on a designated target position. The Dragon captures by landing directly on the target, so this method handles the immediate removal of the captured piece.
      Specified by:
      stab in interface Stabbable
      Parameters:
      target - the position being attacked
      board - the current game board
      Returns:
      the captured piece that was removed from the board

    • getSymbol

      public String getSymbol()
      Retrieves the visual symbol representing the Dragon on the board.
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in interface Moveable
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in class Piece
      Returns:
      the string "D"
